title.localized: Concession & Contrast (aunque, sin embargo, a pesar de)

objectives: Use aunque + indicative and aunque + subjunctive for different meanings
Apply sin embargo, no obstante, a pesar de que correctly
Form complex sentences that acknowledge opposing ideas
Build more nuanced and sophisticated argumentation

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Lorena
dialogue_blocks.text: Aunque llueva mañana, vamos a hacer la excursión igual.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Even if it rains tomorrow, we're going on the excursion anyway.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [ˈauŋke ˈʝweβa maˈɲana ‖ ˈβamos a aˈθeɾ la ekskuɾˈsjon iˈɣwal]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Felipe
dialogue_blocks.text: Aunque hace frío hoy, los niños quieren ir al parque.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Even though it's cold today, the kids want to go to the park.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [ˈauŋke ˈaθe ˈfɾio ˈoj ‖ los ˈniɲos ˈkjeɾen ˈiɾ al ˈpaɾke]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Lorena
dialogue_blocks.text: Está bien. A pesar del frío, es bueno que salgan un rato.
dialogue_blocks.translation: That's fine. Despite the cold, it's good that they go out for a while.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [esˈta ˈβjen ‖ a peˈsaɾ ðel ˈfɾio ‖ es ˈβweno ke ˈsalɣan un ˈɾato]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Felipe
dialogue_blocks.text: No obstante, hay que llevarles ropa abrigadora.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Nevertheless, we need to bring them warm clothes.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [no obsˈtante ‖ aj ke ʝeˈβaɾles ˈropa aβɾiˈɣaðoɾa]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Lorena
dialogue_blocks.text: Cierto. A pesar de que son pequeños, el frío los afecta mucho.
dialogue_blocks.translation: True. Despite the fact that they're little, the cold affects them a lot.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [ˈθjeɾto ‖ a peˈsaɾ ðe ke son peˈkeɲos ‖ el ˈfɾio los aˈfekta ˈmuʧo]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Felipe
dialogue_blocks.text: Sin embargo, si no salen, se aburren y se ponen de mal humor.
dialogue_blocks.translation: However, if they don't go out, they get bored and become grumpy.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [sin emˈβaɾɣo ‖ si no ˈsalen ‖ se aˈβuɾɾen i se ˈponen ðe ˈmal uˈmoɾ]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Lorena
dialogue_blocks.text: Por eso mismo, aunque sea difícil, hay que encontrar el equilibrio.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Precisely because of that, even if it's difficult, one must find the balance.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [poɾ ˈeso ˈmismo ‖ ˈauŋke ˈsea diˈfiθil ‖ aj ke eŋkontɾaɾ el ekiliˈβɾjo]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Felipe
dialogue_blocks.text: Exacto. Que salgan media hora. No es mucho, pero es suficiente.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Exactly. Let them go out for half an hour. It's not much, but it's enough.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [eksˈakto ‖ ke ˈsalɣan ˈmeðja ˈoɾa ‖ no es ˈmuʧo ˈpeɾo es sufiˈθjente]

srs_flashcards.back: even though (known fact): Aunque hace calor, llevo abrigo.
despite + noun: a pesar del frío
no matter how much: Por más que estudie, no apruebo.

exercises.instruction: Escoge la forma correcta de aunque
exercises.question: You don't know whether it will rain. Which is correct: 'We'll go even if it rains'?

feedback.correct: ¡Perfecto! 'Aunque llueva' (subjunctive) because it's hypothetical — we don't know if it will rain. If we knew it was raining right now, we'd say 'aunque llueve'.
feedback.incorrect: When the situation after 'aunque' is hypothetical or uncertain, use the subjunctive: 'aunque llueva'. If you know it's raining, use indicative: 'aunque llueve'.

exercises.instruction: Une el conector con su uso
Completa con el conector de concesión correcto

dialogue.speaker: Rosi
dialogue.text: _____ el precio es alto, vale la pena el viaje. (aunque — known fact)

dialogue.speaker: Juan
dialogue.text: Sí, _____ el costo, la experiencia es única. (a pesar de)

dialogue.speaker: Rosi
dialogue.text: Tienes razón. _____, deberíamos buscar una opción más económica. (sin embargo)

dialogue.speaker: Juan
dialogue.text: _____ encuentres algo más barato, yo iría de todas formas. (aunque — hypothetical)

speaking_rubric.title: Conceder y contrastar
speaking_rubric.scenario: Discuss a topic where you have mixed feelings — something with real advantages AND disadvantages. Use concession structures to show both sides fairly.
speaking_rubric.prompt: Habla de algo que tiene ventajas y desventajas usando aunque, sin embargo, a pesar de.

scoring_criteria.description: Correct indicative/subjunctive after aunque, other connectors

sample_response.text: Aunque me encanta vivir en la ciudad, a veces extraño la tranquilidad del campo. A pesar de que hay mucho entretenimiento aquí, el ruido y el tráfico son agotadores. Sin embargo, aun así no cambiaría la ciudad por nada.
sample_response.ipa: [ˈauŋke me ˈeŋkanta ˈβiβiɾ en la θjuˈðað ‖ a ˈβeθes esˈtɾaɲo la tɾaŋkiliˈðað]

cultural_notes.formality: Mexican academic and formal writing relies heavily on concession structures. University entrance exams and professional communications expect students to demonstrate 'aunque' with both indicative and subjunctive.
cultural_notes.gestures: Tilting the hand from side to side ('sí pero no' gesture) naturally accompanies concession language.
cultural_notes.regional_variations: 'Sin embargo' is formal across all dialects. In casual Mexican speech, 'pero' handles most concession work. 'A pesar de que' is widely understood and used across all Spanish-speaking regions.
